sections.paragraph:
καταπίμπρημι aor. 3 sg. κατέπρησεν (πίμπρημι ‘burn, burn up’; Polyb., Polyaenus+; Philo, Div. Rer. Her. 296; Joseph.; Sb 8246, 46 [IV a.d.]; B-D-F §101, 69) burn to ashes κατέπ[ρ]ησεν as v.l. from P72 mg. in 2 Pt 2:6.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 524
